SEARCH
Package server.scheduler

Class SchedulerConfig

java.lang.Object
server.scheduler.SchedulerConfig
public final class SchedulerConfig extends Object
Timed-job thresholds, read once from the .env file (via DotEnv) with sensible production defaults baked in. Every value is overridable by an environment key so the same build serves both real operation and the live defense demo.

Shrink the .env values for the live demo. The real defaults are measured in hours/minutes; a defense cannot wait an hour for a grab window to lapse. Set e.g. GRAB_WINDOW_MINUTES=1 (and a small SCHEDULER_POLL_SECONDS) in .env and restart the server so an offered grab expires in a minute and the sweep visibly fires.

Recognised keys (with defaults):

  • REMINDER_LEAD_HOURS = 24 — how far ahead the (session b) reminder fires
  • CONFIRM_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 120 — PENDING auto-expiry window (session b)
  • GRAB_WINDOW_MINUTES = 60 — how long an offered waiting-list grab stays claimable
  • SCHEDULER_POLL_SECONDS = 30 — how often every job re-checks

Values are read at class load (after DotEnv.load() has run at server startup) and cached, so a .env edit takes effect on the next restart.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static int
    getConfirmTimeoutMinutes()
    How long a PENDING reservation may sit before auto-expiry, in minutes (session b).
    static int
    getGrabWindowMinutes()
    How long an offered waiting-list grab stays claimable, in minutes.
    static int
    getPollSeconds()
    How often the scheduler re-runs every registered job, in seconds.
    static int
    getReminderLeadHours()
    How far ahead of a visit the confirmation reminder fires, in hours (session b).
